---
title: "Updates broken Ubuntu 12.04.1"
date: 2013-03-31
forum: General Help
---

### Post by dlmille on 2013-03-31
I have been studying the forum and running several suggested solutions  but everything gets back to the same problem -"  linux-headers-3.2.0-34-generic-pae is not loaded".  This file does not  seem to be availalable on the Ubuntu download site and without it I'm  getting nowhere.

I notice I have a 32 bit system and some linux-headers loaded on my system show 64 bit.
[IMG]http://ubuntuforums.org/image/png;base64,[/IMG]

The following package was automatically installed and is no longer required:
  linux-headers-3.2.0-34
Use 'apt-get autoremove' to remove them.
The following extra packages will be installed:
  linux-headers-generic-pae
The following packages will be upgraded:
  linux-headers-generic-pae
1 upgraded, 0 newly installed, 0 to remove and 262 not upgraded.
1 not fully installed or removed.
Need to get 0 B/2,598 B of archives.
After this operation, 0 B of additional disk space will be used.
Do you want to continue [Y/n]? y
dpkg: dependency problems prevent configuration of linux-headers-generic-pae:
 linux-headers-generic-pae depends on linux-headers-3.2.0-34-generic-pae; however:
  Package linux-headers-3.2.0-34-generic-pae is not installed.
dpkg: error processing linux-headers-generic-pae (--configure):
 dependency problems - leaving unconfigured
No apport report written because the error message indicates its a followup error from a previous failure.
                          Errors were encountered while processing:
 linux-headers-generic-pae
E: Sub-process /usr/bin/dpkg returned an error code (1)
dm@dm-OptiPlex-755:~$ sudo apt-get linux-headers-3.2.0-34-generic-pae
E: Invalid operation linux-headers-3.2.0-34-generic-pae
dm@dm-OptiPlex-755:~$ sudo apt-get clean

dm@dm-OptiPlex-755:~$ sudo apt-get update

dm@dm-OptiPlex-755:~$ sudo apt-get --reinstall indicator-messages
E: Invalid operation indicator-messages
dm@dm-OptiPlex-755:~$ sudo apt-get --reinstall install indicator-messages
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
You might want to run 'apt-get -f install' to correct these:
The following packages have unmet dependencies:
 linux-headers-generic-pae : Depends: linux-headers-3.2.0-34-generic-pae but it is not going to be installed
E: Unmet dependencies. Try 'apt-get -f install' with no packages (or specify a solution).
dm@dm-OptiPlex-755:~$ sudo apt-get -f

Usage: apt-get [options] command
       apt-get [options] install|remove pkg1 [pkg2 ...]
       apt-get [options] source pkg1 [pkg2 ...]

ii  linux-headers-3.2.0-23                 3.2.0-23.36                             Header files related to Linux kernel version 3.2.0
ii  linux-headers-3.2.0-23-generic-pae     3.2.0-23.36                             Linux kernel headers for version 3.2.0 on 64 bit x86 SMP
